performance_overview: Through 50 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull now holds 6 open positions after CMG stopped out today. Account equity ~$99,722 (-0.28% since $100K inception) vs SPY -1.40% cumulative — Bull still leads the benchmark by ~1.13pts. Realized record moves to 10 round-trips (3W/7L on the trend book + legacy). Today (Jul 22 EOD) was a rare flat/risk-off session where Bull's defensive book slightly BEAT the market: net +$17.71 (+0.02%) vs SPY -0.05% for +0.07% alpha — the mirror image of yesterday's -0.92% defensive-lag day. The headline event: CMG's GTC 2xATR hard stop ($32.27) filled automatically at 3:02 PM ET (289 shares @ $32.27), realizing -$817.71 (-8.06%) on a 12-day hold. This is exactly the designed behavior — CMG was in earnings blackout (Jul 15-29) with the GTC stop as its SOLE exit, and it executed cleanly at the broker with no manual intervention. Even absorbing that loss, equity ticked up on the day as the surviving book firmed. Unrealized book is net-positive and broadly green: PCG new book leader +5.50% (up from +2.32%), BMY +3.51%, SCHW +2.71% (recovered from yesterday's give-back), GIS +2.71%, XLU RECOVERED to +0.64% (from -1.70%, no longer tightest-to-stop). The lone deteriorating name is META -5.53% (rolled hard from -1.96%), now the CLOSEST position to its stop at only +1.80% above GTC $613.58 — the extended-entry digestion range is breaking lower and it is the top death-cross / stop-out watch.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket for the LEGACY intraday RSI strategy; n=2, thin sample, informational only now that the strategy is MA-20/60 trend-following. Do not act on it without n>=10.

Shallow-cross probation entries continue to hold up under trend-following: GIS (0.29% cross, 75% unit) still +2.71% on Day 8, and BMY (0.13% cross, 50% unit) +3.51% on Day 6 — both green and above their stops after easing from earlier peaks. Sustained positive read that a heavy size-down (NOT a skip) is the right call for shallow-but-non-earnings-adjacent golden crosses. Still only 2-3 samples; keep sizing down, do not upsize yet.
avoid: Shallow EMA cross entries on earnings-adjacent names: TSLA (Jul 7 entry, 0.06% cross spread, earnings Jul 22) lost -4.78% on exit. Rule: SKIP (do not size down) when BOTH shallow cross spread (<0.5%) AND earnings within 14 days apply simultaneously. This is the live rule under the current trend strategy.

Extended entries (price well above EMA-20 at entry): META entered Jul 15 at ~8.5% above EMA-20, peaked +2.83%, chopped in a digestion range for a week, and has now rolled to -5.53% — the digestion finally broke LOWER, and META sits only +1.80% above its stop as the top stop-out watch. The early caution is now confirming: chasing an extended golden cross bought the pullback AND is now bleeding toward the stop. Treat large above-EMA extension (>~6-8%) as a firm size-down or wait-for-pullback rule. n=1 but strengthening.

Very shallow golden crosses (<0.1% EMA-20/60 spread): NVDA (0.07%) skipped Jul 15 as a near-clone of the failed TSLA pattern (0.06%). Treat sub-0.1% crosses as noise-level, high reversal risk — skip on sight. BMY at 0.13% cleared this line by a hair, taken at half unit, and remains green (+3.51%), validating 0.1% as the skip floor.

Earnings-blackout holds ride to the hard stop with no discretionary exit: CMG (entered Jul 10, earnings blackout Jul 15-29) drifted from -4.56% to its 2xATR stop and filled automatically at -8.06% today. This is the blackout policy working as intended — no death-cross override during blackout means the full 2xATR is the risk. Noted as a record of realized blackout downside, not a rule change: the GTC stop is the point of the policy.

LEGACY intraday RSI buckets (informational, strategy retired): RSI 60-65 0/3, RSI 65-75 0/1, gap-up opens with RSI>60 — all losers historically. Retained only as a record of why the intraday RSI approach was abandoned in favor of trend-following.
recent_market_context: Jul 16-22: MA-20/60 trend strategy running; book trimmed from 7 to 6 positions today on CMG's automatic stop-out. Broad market whipsawed: three soft-to-down sessions, a decisive risk-on rally Jul 21 (SPY +0.85%) where Bull's defensive tilt lagged hard (-0.92% alpha), then a flat/mildly-down Jul 22 (SPY -0.05%) where the defensive book edged ahead (+0.07% alpha). No new golden crosses have cleared filters for many consecutive scans (14 free slots, zero queued candidates). One death-cross-equivalent risk building: META rolling toward its stop. VIX last read 15.67 (calm) Jul 16; the choppy two-sided tape since implies VIX remains low-to-moderate. Trending/choppy: choppy, no sustained direction — sharp up-day followed immediately by a flat give-back.

current_open_positions.notes: Day 16; stop $93.28 (fixed, 2xATR). Recovered from yesterday's give-back to +2.71%, ~7.8% above stop. Full unit, no death cross. Anchor position behaving normally.
Day 16; stop $16.29. NEW BOOK LEADER at +5.50% (up from +2.32%), ~10.9% above stop. Steadiest uptrend in the book. Full unit, no death cross.

Day 16; stop $44.37. RECOVERED from -1.70% to +0.64% as utilities firmed on the flat/risk-off day — NO LONGER the tightest-to-stop (now ~3.6% above GTC $44.37). Oldest position, finally back to green but still weak trend. No death cross. Full unit.
Day 8; stop $34.41. Steady green +2.71%, ~7.1% above stop. 75% unit (207 of 276) shallow-cross probation, graduating well. Keep holding, do not upsize.

Day 6; stop $613.58 (2xATR=$23.805). TOP STOP-OUT WATCH — rolled hard from -1.96% to -5.53%, the extended-entry (8.5% above EMA-20) digestion range has now BROKEN LOWER. Only +1.80% above GTC $613.58, the closest position to its stop, overtaking XLU. Watch tonight's scan for a META death cross; GTC handles the exit automatically if it hits $613.58. 75% unit (11 of 15).

Day 6; stop $55.76 (2xATR=1.4723). Green +3.51%, ~9.0% above stop. 50% unit (85 of 171 full), heavy size-down on the shallowest cross (0.13% EMA20/60). Shallow-cross size-down continues to validate. No death cross.
